output.title: Landlord Withholding Security Deposit? Use This 3-Email Demand Sequence (Free Templates)
output.meta_description: Is your landlord not returning your deposit? Copy our free security deposit dispute letter email sequence. State deadlines, tenant rights & recovery tactics.
content_outline: Introduction: My Landlord Won't Give Me My Deposit Back — What Are My Rights?
Email 1 Template: The Initial Demand — Subject: 'Re: [Address] — Security Deposit Not Returned'
Email 2 Template: The Legal Reminder — Subject: 'Deadline Passed: Tenant Rights Security Deposit Return'
Email 3 Template: Final Notice — Subject: 'Final Notice: Security Deposit Dispute Letter & Next Steps'
What Can a Landlord Deduct From Security Deposit? (And What They Can't)
What to Do When Landlord Doesn't Return Deposit After 30 Days
key_points: Landlords are required by state law to return security deposits within 14–60 days depending on jurisdiction
A written demand letter is often the legally required first step before small claims court and significantly increases recovery rates
The 3-email sequence escalates from polite inquiry to formal legal notice, citing specific tenant rights and state deadlines
Normal wear and tear cannot be deducted; landlords must provide an itemized list of any lawful deductions
DepositBack automates state-specific, legally compliant demand letters in under 5 minutes
output.cta: Stop chasing your landlord. Generate a state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack and recover your money in days—not months.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters whose landlord is withholding, delaying, or refusing to return a security deposit and need urgent, legally sound recovery templates
